_comment: V4.0 Phase 1 — Kundli Matching (Ashtakoota / Guna Milan) reference data. Consumed by services/astrology/kundliMatchingEngine.js via ruleLoader.js, following the exact same config-driven convention as houses.json / planetaryFriendship.json / nakshatraProfile.json. Gana/Yoni/Nadi per-nakshatra facts are intentionally NOT duplicated here — the engine reads them straight from the existing rules/nakshatraProfile.json (via nakshatraProfileEngine.js) so the two features can never drift apart. This file only adds the additional lookup tables that single-chart nakshatraProfile.json has no reason to carry: Varna, Vashya, Yoni compatibility, Gana compatibility, Graha Maitri compatibility, and Bhakoot distance rules.

varna._comment: Classical Varna (spiritual temperament tier) by Moon-sign. 1 point (Varna Koota) if the groom's Varna rank is >= the bride's Varna rank, else 0.

vashya._comment: Vashya (mutual control/attraction) group by Moon-sign. This engine works at whole-sign resolution (no divisional degree data — see planetPositionEngine.js), so signs that classically split across two Vashya groups by degree are assigned to their commonly-used simplified whole-sign group.

tara._comment: Tara (birth-star counting) Koota. Computed from the two people's Nakshatra index (not sign-based, no table needed) — see kundliMatchingEngine.js#calcTaraKoota. Counting from each person's Nakshatra to the other's (mod 9) gives a Tara number 1-9; numbers 3, 5, and 7 (Vipat, Pratyak, Vadha) are inauspicious. Each direction is worth 1.5 of the 3 total points.

yoni._comment: Yoni (intimate/animal-instinct) compatibility Koota — the animal per Nakshatra is read from rules/nakshatraProfile.json (14 classical Yonis). Simplified to 3 tiers (Same/Enemy/Neutral) rather than a full 14x14 friend-tier matrix, since this engine's whole-sign/whole-nakshatra resolution (see constants.js) does not track finer distinctions real Panchang software uses — the 7 classical Vairi (great-enemy) opposite pairs below are the one part of the Yoni table every tradition agrees on, so those are kept precise and everything else is treated as Neutral.

gana._comment: Gana (temperament: Deva/divine, Manushya/human, Rakshasa/demonic) Koota — Gana per Nakshatra is read from rules/nakshatraProfile.json. Classical asymmetric groom-row/bride-column scoring table.

grahaMaitri._comment: Graha Maitri (planetary friendship between the two Moon-sign lords) Koota. Reuses the existing Naisargika Maitri table (rules/planetaryFriendship.json) in both directions, then combines the pair of relations below. Same lord for both charts scores the maximum (a sign is always maximally friendly with itself).

bhakoot._comment: Bhakoot (overall Moon-sign house-distance) Koota. Counting the bride's Moon-sign forward from the groom's Moon-sign (groom's own sign = 1), distances of 2, 12 (Dwirdwadash), 5, 9 (Navapancham), or 6, 8 (Shadashtak) are classically inauspicious and score 0; every other distance scores the full 7.

nadi._comment: Nadi (life-pulse/genetic-lineage) Koota — the highest-weighted single Koota. Nadi per Nakshatra is read from rules/nakshatraProfile.json (Vata/Pitta/Kapha — the same tripartite classification classical texts call Aadi/Madhya/Antya Nadi). Same Nadi for both people is Nadi Dosha (0); different Nadi scores the full 8.
